objective: Make the Weekly operating board visibly useful from the Today first screen by showing the browser-local weekly summary and providing a real Today action that writes the weekly plan into local state.
riskClass: Q3 because this run includes production deployment/promotion after local proof; app code itself is Q2 browser-local frontend state.
approvalStatus: Production promotion was previously approved by user message: PRODUCTION_DEPLOY_APPROVED=true promote. This slice does not edit credentials, auth, DNS, backend services, n8n, Telegram, Postgres, public MCP, or WHOOP settings.
truthBoundary: The Today weekly command is browser-local demo behavior only.
No WHOOP, accounts, hosted AI, Telegram, n8n, public MCP, Postgres, backend writes, reminders, timers, or cross-device sync are connected or claimed.
The separate LifeMax lab/source boot reports remain red, so connected daily usability is not claimed.
defectsFound: The prior weekly-board slice worked from Patterns, but a user landing on /today still had to discover the higher-level board elsewhere.
The Today first screen did not visibly summarize the weekly board after loading the sample week.
Standalone Playwright/Chromium timed out launching after 180 seconds in this desktop environment; in-app browser proof was required instead.
Initial in-app browser smoke selectors were too strict for exposed accessibility/input-value behavior and were repaired before proof was accepted.
repairsMade: Added TodayWeeklyBoardSummary inside the Today command center when the derived weekly_board is ready.
Added a Use week plan action on Today that calls createPlanFromWeeklyBoard, clears any focus session, resets plan_done, creates a local memory candidate, and shows a role=status message.
Added compact CSS for the Today weekly summary without nesting another full panel/card.
Extended the sample-week visual test so the Today-first weekly-board block and action are asserted before navigating to Patterns.

localChecks.detail: Next.js 16.2.7 static build generated 10 routes including /today, /capture, /patterns, /experiments, /profile, /privacy
browserType.launch timed out after 180000ms before app assertions; interrupted second test. This was not accepted as product proof.

localChecks.command: rg secret-pattern scan over changed source/test files

browserLocal.server: npm run start -- --hostname 127.0.0.1 --port 3000 after production build
flow: Open /profile and reset browser-local demo state.
Open /today and click Load sample week.
Verify Today weekly board summary is visible and says Weekly board ready from local history.
Click Use week plan from Today.
Verify status message says the weekly board cue moved into Today's browser-local plan.
Verify Daily plan fields: Must-do Protect first useful block, Optional move 2 Close and archive one more local day, Avoid today Do not start with messages before plan.
Refresh /today and verify the plan and Today weekly board persist.
Open /capture and verify three capture items, including protected first block and messages before plan.
Open /patterns and verify Weekly operating board plus 3-checkpoint local trend.
Open /privacy and verify privacy policy plus WHOOP disconnected-services copy.
Open /today at 1280x800 and verify no horizontal overflow.

labBoundary.bootPacket: red at 2026-06-20T12:23:52.887Z: safe_to_claim_daily_usable false; local preflight red; stale shared-context reports

residualRisks: This is still browser-local demo behavior; real backend/account/integration readiness is unverified and currently red in lab boot artifacts.
Standalone Playwright remains unreliable in this desktop process state; in-app browser proof is the accepted visual evidence for this checkpoint.
Production smoke is still pending in this packet and must be added before claiming the deployed slice is proven.
rollbackPath: Git rollback: revert the Today weekly command commit and redeploy the previous production commit if needed.
Runtime rollback: promote the previous Vercel production deployment. No database/auth/credential migration is part of this slice.
User-data rollback is not needed for backend records because this feature writes only browser-local demo state.
